steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: one explains that the units measured area and length, and the other says neither unit is commonly used today. Respectively belongs with area and length, so it needs a comma before it and a stronger boundary after it.

steps.title: Reject misplaced boundaries
steps.content:
• Choice A places the semicolon before respectively, separating the word from the pair it explains.
  • Choice B creates a fused sentence between two independent clauses.
  • Choice D uses only a comma between independent clauses, creating a comma splice.
The answer is C.
